title: Bakery Website
summary: Marketing site for a bakery with menu highlights, hours, and contact—fast, accessible, and easy to update.

body.raw:
# Project overview

A **content-forward** bakery site emphasizing photography, seasonal menus, and clear calls to action (order, visit, contact). Built for performance (static generation where possible), accessibility, and simple deployment.

## Highlights

- **Responsive layout** tuned for mobile visitors discovering the shop on the go.
- **Structured content** for menu sections and location details.
- **SEO-friendly** metadata and semantic HTML.

## Next steps

Replace the placeholder hero with real photography, and set `liveUrl` and/or `repoUrl` in frontmatter when the site is published.

title: Reaction-Time Flappy Bird Game (Embedded Systems)
summary: Real-time Flappy Bird-style game implemented on Terasic DE10-Standard SoC FPGA board, demonstrating embedded hardware/software co-design with C, Verilog, and AXI-Lite communication.

body.raw:
# Project Overview

> **🎮 Embedded Gaming System**
> 
> This project implements a reaction-based "Flappy Bird"–style game on the Terasic DE10-Standard SoC FPGA board, demonstrating embedded hardware/software co-design. The game challenges players to control a bird navigating through dynamically generated obstacles using physical push buttons, switches, and real-time visual feedback.
> 
> The system showcases the integration of real-time embedded software, digital logic design, and system-on-chip architecture, serving as a capstone example of embedded co-design principles.

## 🎯 Key Features

- **Hardware/Software Co-Design:** HPS executes game logic while FPGA manages low-level I/O operations

- **Real-Time Performance:** 60 Hz gameplay loop with consistent frame rate and deterministic timing

- **Multi-Domain Communication:** AXI-Lite register interface enables seamless HPS↔FPGA synchronization

- **Hardware Input Processing:** Debounced inputs, LED indicators, and 7-segment display updates

- **Visual Feedback System:** 128×64 SPI LCD display with PWM-based backlight control

- **Collision Detection:** Real-time physics with scoring and adjustable difficulty via board switches

---

# 🏗️ Technical Architecture

## 🔧 Hardware Platform

- **Terasic DE10-Standard:** Intel Cyclone V SoC FPGA with dual-core ARM Cortex-A9 HPS
- **Display:** 128×64 SPI LCD for game rendering
- **Input/Output:** Push buttons, slide switches, user LEDs, 7-segment display

## 💻 Software Stack

- **HPS Domain:** C programming for game logic, physics, and rendering loop
- **FPGA Domain:** Verilog for low-level I/O management and register interface
- **Development Tools:** Quartus Prime, Platform Designer (Qsys), GCC toolchain

## 🔄 System Architecture

```
HPS (C) ←→ AXI-Lite Bridge ←→ FPGA (Verilog)
    ↓                              ↓
Game Logic & Physics          I/O Management
Rendering Loop (60Hz)         Input Debouncing
SPI LCD Output               LED Control
                           7-Segment Display
                           PWM Backlight
```
![OpenGL rendering example](/images/projects/component_diagram.png)

## 📡 Communication Protocol

- **Shared Registers:** Synchronized game states between HPS and FPGA domains
- **AXI-Lite Interface:** Low-latency communication for real-time control
- **SPI Communication:** High-speed LCD frame buffer updates

---

# 🎮 Game Implementation

## 🎯 Core Gameplay

- **Reaction-Based Control:** Player uses push buttons to control bird movement
- **Dynamic Obstacles:** Procedurally generated pipes with adjustable difficulty
- **Real-Time Physics:** Collision detection and scoring system
- **Visual Feedback:** LED indicators and 7-segment display for game state

## ⚡ Performance Optimization

- **Hardware-Timed Loop:** Consistent 60 Hz frame rate with deterministic timing
- **Efficient Rendering:** Optimized SPI communication for smooth graphics
- **Low-Latency Input:** Hardware-based input debouncing for responsive controls

## 🧪 Testing & Validation

- **Structured Test Plan:** Comprehensive validation of HPS↔FPGA communication
- **Frame Integrity Testing:** Verification of SPI rendering and display output
- **Collision Logic Testing:** Systematic validation of game physics
- **System Responsiveness:** Performance testing under various load conditions

---

# 🎯 Impact & Outcomes

## ✅ Technical Achievements

- **Successful Integration:** Seamless HPS and FPGA domain communication via AXI-Lite
- **Real-Time Performance:** Achieved consistent 60 Hz gameplay with hardware timing
- **Hardware Optimization:** Efficient I/O management with debounced inputs and PWM control
- **System Reliability:** Robust error handling and deterministic timing across domains

## 🌟 Key Learning Outcomes

- **FPGA Development:** Digital logic design and Verilog programming for embedded systems
- **HPS Interfacing:** ARM Cortex-A9 programming and system integration
- **AXI Bridge Communication:** Low-level hardware communication protocols
- **Embedded Co-Design:** Integration of software and hardware domains in SoC architecture
- **Real-Time Systems:** Deterministic timing and performance optimization for embedded applications

## 🔧 Technical Skills Demonstrated

- **Hardware Description Languages:** Verilog for FPGA logic implementation
- **Embedded C Programming:** Real-time systems programming on ARM architecture
- **System Integration:** SoC design and inter-domain communication
- **Hardware Debugging:** Low-level system testing and validation methodologies

---

> **💡 Project Significance**
> 
> This embedded systems project demonstrates mastery of hardware/software co-design principles, showcasing the integration of real-time embedded software with digital logic design. The Flappy Bird implementation serves as a comprehensive example of SoC architecture, AXI communication protocols, and embedded systems development, highlighting expertise in FPGA programming, HPS interfacing, and real-time system optimization.

title: Phytopi Controlled Environment Platform
summary: Autonomous plant growth monitoring system using Raspberry Pi, Flutter apps, Supabase telemetry, and ML-based plant health insights.

body.raw:
# Project Overview

> **🌱 Smart IoT Ecosystem**
> 
> PhytoPi is a smart IoT ecosystem designed to automate and monitor plant growth in controlled environments. The system integrates hardware sensors, Raspberry Pi control, cloud data management, and mobile/web applications to deliver real-time environmental insights and plant health analytics.
> 
> The project's goal is to autonomously sustain a full bean plant growth cycle while providing users with live monitoring, alerts, and predictive analysis through a unified app interface.

## 🎯 Key Features

- **Environmental Monitoring:** Continuous data collection for soil moisture, temperature, humidity, light, and CO₂ sensors

- **Secure Cloud Infrastructure:** Built on Supabase with Row-Level Security (RLS) for user-specific data isolation

- **Cross-Platform Dashboard:** Flutter app (Android/iOS/Web) displays real-time readings, charts, and device health

- **Alert System:** Automated notifications for threshold breaches (e.g., low moisture, high temperature)

- **Offline-Resilient Device Agent:** Python-based Raspberry Pi agent caches data locally and uploads in batches

- **AI Integration (Phase 2):** On-device TensorFlow Lite or cloud ML microservice for plant health inference

- **Kiosk Mode:** Raspberry Pi can display live dashboards using Chromium in kiosk mode for lab or greenhouse use

- **Monetization-Ready Architecture:** Stripe integration for subscription tiers and device management

---

# 🏗️ Technical Architecture

## 🔧 Hardware Layer

- **Raspberry Pi 5** + Sensor Array (moisture, temp, humidity, light, CO₂)

- **Device Agent:** Python + SQLite for local buffering, secure upload to Supabase Edge Functions

## ☁️ Backend Infrastructure

- **Supabase:** PostgreSQL, RLS, Edge Functions, Storage, Auth

- **CI/CD:** GitHub Actions for migrations, tests, and deployment

## 📱 Frontend Applications

- **Flutter:** Mobile + Web, realtime charts, user settings, notifications

- **Kiosk Mode:** Raspberry Pi displays live dashboards using Chromium

## 🔄 Data Flow

```
Sensors → Pi Agent → Edge Function → Supabase DB → Realtime → Mobile/Web UI
```

---

## 📁 Project Structure

```
phytopi/
├─ apps/            # Flutter mobile + web dashboard clients
├─ services/        # pi-agent, simulator, ml microservice
├─ ml/              # training scripts, datasets, exported TFLite models
├─ infra/           # Supabase migrations, edge functions, Docker assets
├─ packages/        # shared DTOs, domain models, utilities
├─ .github/workflows/# CI/CD pipelines
└─ docs/            # requirements, diagrams, statements of work
```

# 🎯 Impact & Outcomes

## ✅ Achievements

- **Fully functional MVP** achieved within 12 weeks

- **Cross-platform app** + Raspberry Pi kiosk mode tested with live sensors

- **Secure, scalable Supabase backend** supporting realtime ingestion and notifications

- **Architecture ready** for production and future AI health analysis

## 🌟 Key Highlights

- **End-to-end IoT system** with device onboarding, telemetry, and visualization

- **Modular Supabase schema** designed for extensibility and manufacturing integration

- **Demonstrates strong grasp** of IoT, cloud computing, mobile development, and embedded systems

- **Deployed internal tests** using CI/CD pipelines and Supabase observability tools

---

> **💡 Vision Statement**
> 
> PhytoPi represents a comprehensive IoT solution that bridges hardware, cloud infrastructure, and user experience to create an intelligent plant monitoring ecosystem. The project demonstrates expertise across the full technology stack while delivering a production-ready system with clear paths for AI integration and commercial scalability.

title: RimWorld Gameplay Mod
summary: Custom RimWorld mod extending colony simulation with new behaviors, defs, and Harmony patches—built with C# against the RimWorld modding API.

body.raw:
# Project overview

This mod extends **RimWorld** with custom gameplay elements using the game’s def system, C# assemblies, and **Harmony** for non-destructive patches. Work focuses on balancing new mechanics with vanilla systems and keeping saves compatible across updates.

## Highlights

- **Defs and data-driven content** for items, jobs, and behaviors where possible.
- **Harmony patches** to hook into vanilla logic without forking core code.
- **Testing** in dev mode with logging to catch edge cases early.

## Next steps

Swap `heroImage` for a screenshot or Workshop banner, and add `repoUrl`, `liveUrl` (e.g. Steam Workshop), or `extraLinks` in frontmatter when ready.

title: School Hallway 3D Scene (OpenGL)
summary: Interactive 3D school hallway scene built with OpenGL, C++, and GLUT for Computer Graphics coursework, featuring realistic lighting, textures, and 3D object modeling.

body.raw:
# Project Overview

> **🎨 3D Computer Graphics Scene**
> 
> A comprehensive 3D school hallway scene implemented in OpenGL using C++ and GLUT. This project demonstrates advanced computer graphics concepts including 3D object modeling, lighting, texturing, and camera positioning in a realistic school environment.
![OpenGL rendering example](/images/projects/hallway_irl.png)
## 🎯 Key Features

- **3D Object Modeling:** Detailed 3D models of chairs, tables, lighting fixtures, and architectural elements
- **Realistic Lighting:** Multiple light sources including ceiling lamps and ambient lighting
- **Interactive Camera:** GLUT-based camera controls for scene navigation
- **Modular Architecture:** Object-oriented design with separate rendering functions for each 3D element
- **Cross-Platform Build:** Makefile-based build system supporting Linux/Ubuntu environments

## 🏗️ Technical Implementation

### Graphics Pipeline
- **OpenGL Rendering:** Core graphics API for 3D scene rendering
- **GLUT Window Management:** Cross-platform window creation and event handling
- **GLSL Shaders:** Custom vertex and fragment shaders for advanced rendering effects
- **3D Mathematics:** Matrix transformations, camera positioning, and perspective projection

### Scene Components
- **Floor:** Gray-colored floor plane with proper texturing
- **Walls:** Light blue back wall with realistic material properties
- **Furniture:** Brown lounge chairs and green plastic chairs with detailed geometry
- **Lighting:** Yellow ceiling lamp with proper light source positioning
- **Architecture:** Complete hallway layout with proper proportions

## 🔧 Development Environment

### Build System
```bash
# Install dependencies
make install-deps

# Build the project
make

# Build and run
make run
```

### Dependencies
- **build-essential:** GCC compiler and build tools
- **libgl1-mesa-dev:** OpenGL development files
- **libglu1-mesa-dev:** GLU utility library
- **freeglut3-dev:** GLUT library for window management

## 📁 Project Structure

```
SchoolHallwayOpenGL/
├─ src/              # C++ source files
├─ shaders/          # GLSL shader files
├─ Makefile         # Build configuration
└─ README.md        # Documentation
```

## 🎯 Learning Outcomes

### Computer Graphics Concepts
- **3D Modeling:** Understanding of 3D coordinate systems and object positioning
- **Lighting Models:** Implementation of ambient, diffuse, and specular lighting
- **Camera Systems:** Perspective projection and view matrix calculations
- **Rendering Pipeline:** Vertex processing, rasterization, and fragment shading

### Technical Skills
- **OpenGL Programming:** Low-level graphics API usage and optimization
- **C++ Development:** Object-oriented programming for graphics applications
- **Build Systems:** Makefile configuration and dependency management
- **Cross-Platform Development:** Linux/Ubuntu environment setup and configuration

## 🌟 Key Highlights

- **Educational Project:** Developed as part of Computer Graphics coursework
- **Complete 3D Scene:** Fully functional interactive 3D environment
- **Modular Design:** Clean separation of concerns with individual object rendering
- **Professional Build System:** Automated dependency management and compilation
- **Documentation:** Comprehensive README with installation and usage instructions

---

> **💡 Technical Achievement**
> 
> This project demonstrates proficiency in computer graphics programming, 3D mathematics, and OpenGL development. The modular architecture and comprehensive documentation showcase professional software development practices while delivering an engaging 3D visualization experience.

title: Video Editing Pipeline

body.raw:
# Project overview

A **repeatable pipeline** for personal and project video work: standardized ingest, proxies for smooth editing, consistent timeline organization, and export ladders for web and archival masters.

## Highlights

- **Folder templates** and naming so assets stay traceable across revisions.
- **FFmpeg** helpers for transcode, loudness, and batch exports where NLE automation is thin.
- **Preset libraries** for recurring deliverables (e.g. portfolio clips, social cuts).

## Next steps

title: VR Cashier Simulator for Neurodivergent Learners
summary: An immersive virtual reality training environment designed specifically for neurodivergent learners, featuring AI-driven social scenarios where users practice cashier skills in a calm, structured virtual store environment.

body.raw:
# Project Overview

> **🌐 Accessible VR Training**
> 
> The VR Cashier Simulator is an engaging virtual environment designed specifically for neurodivergent learners. Neurodivergence refers to various conditions that affect cognitive functioning, learning, attention, and mood, including autism spectrum disorder, ADHD, dyslexia, dyspraxia, and others.
> 
> This project creates a simulation tailored to the unique needs of neurodivergent learners, emphasizing focus and immersiveness through carefully designed environments and interactions.

## 🎯 Key Features

- **AI-Driven Environment:** Users interact with simulated social scenarios, playing the role of a cashier in a virtual store
- **Calm & Structured Design:** Japanese architecture-inspired main menu creates a peaceful learning environment
- **Interactive Tutorials:** Step-by-step guidance with floating instructional videos and background narration
- **Dynamic AI Customers:** AI selects random items from shelves for players to scan and bag
- **Restocking System:** Complete game cycle includes scanning, bagging, and restocking items
- **Accessibility Focus:** Visual and auditory guidance ensures players understand controls and objectives

---

# 🎮 User Experience

## 🏛️ Main Menu

The environment features Japanese architecture-inspired design, giving it a calm and structured feel. The space includes invisible barriers (colliders) keeping players within the design space. Front and center, there's the title of the game along with an icon representing the store.

The menu is simple, offering two buttons:
- **Initial Tutorial:** Control instructions and VR basics
- **Store Tutorial:** Cashier-specific training

When players first open the app, they automatically begin in the initial tutorial.

## 📚 Initial Tutorial

The initial tutorial keeps things straightforward with visual distinction—originally plain with a white background, now enhanced with a red floor for better visual clarity.

The tutorial starts with a video that walks players through the controls, explaining what each button on the controller does. We chose video format because it's easier for players, especially those new to VR, to understand controls through clear and visual demonstration. The video guides players step-by-step, ensuring they know how to interact with the environment before moving on to the next tutorial.

Once completed, players are automatically taken to the store tutorial.

## 🛒 Store Tutorial

In the store tutorial, players learn how to:
- **Scan and bag items:** Practice the core cashier skills
- **Interact with the scanning machine:** Use the built-in UI for starting and completing transactions
- **Restock shelves:** Return items to their designated spots after transactions

### Game Flow

1. **AI Customer Interaction:** The AI selects random items from the shelves based on a randomizer that pulls from the store's inventory
2. **Scanning & Bagging:** Players scan each item and place them in bags
3. **Transaction Completion:** Players use the scanning machine's UI to finalize transactions
4. **Customer Departure:** Once the transaction is complete, the AI customer leaves the store
5. **Restocking:** Items spawn inside designated boxes, and players must place them on highlighted spots on the shelves
6. **Completion:** After all items are restocked, players can either return to the main menu or restart the tutorial

---

# 🔧 Technical Implementation

## 🎨 Environment Design

- **Collision Detection:** Invisible barriers (colliders) keep players within designated play areas
- **Visual Guidance:** Floating instructional videos guide players throughout each tutorial step
- **Audio Support:** Background narration explains what needs to be done, providing a smooth and accessible experience
- **Intuitive Interactions:** Every action in the VR environment is carefully programmed for efficient interaction

## 🤖 AI System

- **Dynamic Item Selection:** AI randomly selects items from the store's inventory
- **Realistic Customer Behavior:** AI customers interact with shelves and create authentic shopping scenarios
- **Future Enhancement:** Plans to integrate advanced AI using ChatGPT for more realistic and engaging social experiences

## ⚡ Performance & Collaboration

- **Unity Cloud Integration:** Version control was smooth thanks to Unity Cloud
- **Team Collaboration:** Easy division of work without overlapping tasks
- **Optimized Performance:** Game runs smoothly even with complex AI interactions and detailed environments
- **Scalable Architecture:** System designed to support future AI enhancements

---

# 🎯 Impact & Future Plans

## ✅ Current Achievements

- **Accessible Design:** Created an intuitive and educational experience tailored for neurodivergent learners
- **Smooth Gameplay:** Optimized performance ensures enjoyable VR experience
- **Clear Instruction System:** Video-based tutorials and narration make the game accessible to all skill levels
- **Complete Training Cycle:** Full cashier workflow from customer service to restocking

## 🚀 Future Enhancements

- **Advanced AI Integration:** Plans to integrate ChatGPT for more realistic and engaging social experiences
- **Enhanced Immersion:** AI-driven conversations will allow players to interact with customers in more meaningful ways
- **Expanded Scenarios:** Additional training scenarios and difficulty levels

---

> **💡 Project Significance**
> 
> This VR Cashier Simulator represents a commitment to accessible technology and inclusive design. By focusing on the unique needs of neurodivergent learners, the project demonstrates how VR can be a powerful tool for training and skill development when designed with accessibility and user experience in mind. The combination of calm, structured environments, clear visual guidance, and AI-driven scenarios creates an effective learning platform that can help individuals build confidence and practical skills in a safe, controlled environment.
